items.title: 'Short-term pain' to wrestle Tasmanian budget back to black in two years

children.content: There will be "short-term pain" to get Tasmania's finances back in the black within two years, state treasurer Eric Abetz says ahead of today's budget.

gallery.altText: Politicians sitting in a parliament room, listening to one older guy speak.

thumbnail.altText: Politicians sitting in a parliament room, listening to one older guy speak.

items.title: They’re black, white and very cute. But not all gentoo penguins are the same

children.content: Gentoo penguins were thought to be a standalone species, but a new study says there are four distinct species — and it could lead to better conservation plans.

image.altText: Three small penguins with bright red-orange bills and conspicuous white eye patches.

items.title: Government businesses defy premier's call for transparency

children.content: Tasmanian government-owned businesses or companies are being threatened with legislation to force them to disclose right-to-information documents publicly after nine of them failed to respond to the premier's request.

image.altText: Man looks out to the distance, leaning on a green chair

items.title: Complaint from ex-boss led to investigation into embattled mental health clinic

children.content: Tasmania's largest private mental health hospital, which was given a $2 million lifeline by the state government last year, had its corporate governance placed under review, following a formal complaint by the facility's former chief executive.

image.altText: Generic photos of a mental health facility.

items.title: The battle for Tasmania's north has been won, but locals aren't convinced

children.content: They may not be scheduled to play their first AFL game until 2028, but the Tasmania Devils have claimed their first scalp — booting Hawthorn out of the state which has been its second home for more than two decades.

image.altText: Two football players celebrate during a game.

items.title: 'We're not fleeing': The disappearing Pacific nation relocating its people to Australia

children.content: Tuvalu is building new land to hold back rising sea levels, but the majority of its people are applying to relocate to Australia. As they do, many are confronting a disturbing question.

gallery.altText: An aerial photo of land reclamation work in Tuvalu's capital Funafuti.
A drone shot of Tuvalu's only international runway surrounded by ocean.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial photo of land reclamation work in Tuvalu's capital Funafuti.
A drone shot of Tuvalu's only international runway surrounded by ocean.
Officials were surprised when the majority of Tuvalu's population applied for the Falepili mobility scheme.

items.title: Diphtheria is spreading in Australia and so is misinformation about it

children.content: Falsehoods about diphtheria are spreading online as a worsening outbreak of the disease affects Indigenous communities.

image.altText: Falsehoods about diphtheria

items.title: Inside the 'worst managed infrastructure project in Australian history'

children.content: The Inland Rail has had funding slashed and the project placed on pause but Everald Compton has a bottle of Penfolds Grange waiting and even if it takes him till the age of 100, he's determined to see it through.

image.altText: Old man looking at model train.

items.title: Israeli minister taunts detained activists from Global Sumud Flotilla in video

children.content: Some of the detained activists were being pushed and dragged along the ground by Israeli forces while Itamar Ben-Gvir was taunting them.

image.altText: A vertical picture of people whose hands are tied kneeling in a fenced area on a ship

gallery.altText: people kneeling on the ground as armed soldiers watch over

thumbnail.altText: people kneeling on the ground as armed soldiers watch over

items.title: Brothers of Bondi hero ordered to stay away from him after alleged threat

children.content: Two brothers of Ahmed Al Ahmed faced Bankstown Local Court over alleged verbal threats via the phone.

image.altText: Bondi Beach massacre hero Ahmed al Ahmed joins first responders on Day 1 of the Fifth Men’s Ashes Test

items.title: Last two bodies of Italian cave divers recovered

children.content: Finnish divers have recovered the remaining bodies of the divers that died in an underwater cave in the Maldives.

image.altText: A diver is seen on the deck of a boat, preparing to go into the water. Diving equipment is on the floor beside them.

items.title: Can Australia make its own fertiliser without fossil fuels?

children.content: Most fertiliser is a by-product of the oil industry, which is why it's so vulnerable to the current blockade. But there are other sources the industry could eventually switch to.

gallery.altText: Green crops in the foreground with machinery in the background.
Tractor pulling large machine through rows of green crops in a field.

thumbnail.altText: Green crops in the foreground with machinery in the background.
Tractor pulling large machine through rows of green crops in a field.

items.title: San Diego shooters named Australia's worst mass murderer in online posts

children.content: Thousands of kilometres away, but the terrible connection to Australia is evident. ABC NEWS Verify looks at the inspiration behind the San Diego Islamic Centre murders.

image.altText: A group of men in dark clothing gathered and embracing outside next to a yellow crime scene tape.

items.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese meets family of Kumanjayi Little Baby

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says Australia stands with the family of a five-year-old Aboriginal girl, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs last month.

image.altText: Albo has arms around an Indigenous woman wearing scarf, holding pink toy, a woman has head on another's shoulder. All serious.

items.title: Why renovating your outdoor area gives 'the best bang for your buck'

children.content: Adding a new kitchen or bathroom to your home can be expensive, but budget constraints don't mean you have to take home improvement off the table altogether.

gallery.altText: two lush garden beds outside a small cottage.

thumbnail.altText: two lush garden beds outside a small cottage.

items.title: Can 'Baby Yoda' get Star Wars fans back in the cinema?

children.content: Grogu (aka Baby Yoda) made The Mandalorian a hit on streaming, now Disney is looking to parlay that goodwill into ticket sales to the latest Star Wars cinematic release.

image.altText: Four small creatures ride in a cart in the desert

items.title: Think you know music and film? Let's see how well you score in this quiz

children.content: From Mozart to Max Richter, can you pick the classical music in these iconic films?

image.altText: A man in a yellow space suit walks through a white hexagonal corridor on a space station.

items.title: Putin's China visit looked like Trump's but couldn't have been more different

children.content: Vladimir Putin and Xi Jinping agreed to a raft of deals in front of invited press less than a week after Donald Trump left China's capital with no confirmed deals.

items.title: Banks, telcos briefed on threat of 'dangerous' AI model

children.content: A new generation of AI models deemed "too dangerous" for public release is driving Australia's efforts to lure top AI companies to establish a major presence in the country.

image.altText: Anthropic logo is seen in this photo

items.title: Australia's costliest disaster recovery program fails to deliver a single home

children.content: A new report finds a disaster recovery program set up after the devastating 2022 floods in northern NSW is yet to deliver any of the homes it promised.

image.altText: A woman looks out of her first story window as flood water laps at hair stair case, her whole yard covered in brown water.

items.title: Food waste from Queen Victoria Market to help vulnerable Melbournians

children.content: A new program at Melbourne's Queen Victoria Market is tackling the massive amount of food wasted, while helping the rising number of people struggling to put food on the table.

image.altText: Muz wearing a dark hoodie and cap holding a box of tomatos in a market

items.title: Death of Peter Hollingworth sparks difficult feelings for abuse victim

children.content: Former governor-general Peter Hollingworth, who resigned over his handling of child abuse in the church, died in Melbourne on Tuesday at the age of 91.

image.altText: An older man in a suit walking in front of a stone wall

items.title: 'What I need right now isn't sympathy': 30yo Souths star retires after MND diagnosis

children.content: Rabbitohs and Maroons enforcer Jai Arrow announces he will step away from rugby league after being diagnosed with motor neurone disease.

gallery.altText: Jai Arrow at his retirement press conference

thumbnail.altText: Jai Arrow at his retirement press conference

items.title: Alcaraz injury woes deepen, former champion out of Wimbledon

children.content: Carlos Alcaraz will miss Wimbledon as he continues his rehab from a wrist injury that has already ruled him out of the French Open.

image.altText: Carlos Alcaraz claps with his wrist in a cast

items.title: Luai and Tigers headline NRL's Las Vegas quartet for 2027 season-openers

children.content: Jarome Luai will begin his final season with the Wests Tigers in Las Vegas, with the NRL unveiling its teams for next year's season-openers.

image.altText: Jarome Luai lookes dejected after a Wests Tigers' loss during the 2025 NRL season.

items.title: Doctor fined for book of 'WTF' surgery pictures taken without consent

children.content: An anaesthetist who worked at Toowoomba Hospital has been fined $30,000 for professional misconduct after he included confidential patient information in a self-published book.

image.altText: A doctor in scrubs and with a headband is standing in front of a piece of medical equipment.

items.title: Greenland tells US again it is 'not for sale' after Trump envoy talks

children.content: Jeff Landry, appointed by Donald Trump in December as the US special envoy to Greenland, has met the country's prime minister in the capital Nuuk.

image.altText: Lines of snow-covered houses seen on the edge of a snowy coastline, alongside dark ocean water.

items.title: Core Lithium restarts Northern Territory mine as price rallies

children.content: Core Lithium has restarted mining at its project near Darwin as prices surge close to $4,200 a tonne.

gallery.altText: an aerial photo of a mining processing plant.

thumbnail.altText: an aerial photo of a mining processing plant.

items.title: New move to change SA's abortion laws goes before state parliament

children.content: South Australia's parliament is set to debate restricting abortion access for the third time in less than two years, with One Nation's breakthrough election result meaning the laws could pass the upper house.

image.altText: Sarah Game smiles next to a group of rally attendees outside a large grey building

items.title: Paedophile former magistrate to be released on home detention

children.content: Paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy will be released from jail when his sentence expires next month, despite an ongoing application to have him indefinitely detained.

image.altText: Notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy.

items.title: Property developers investing in 'farms for children', childcare report finds

children.content: For-profit providers have been accused of putting growth and revenue ahead of quality care in a report into childcare safety failures.

image.altText: A non-identifiable child playing with toys.

items.title: New Qld public hospital to refuse some services for religious reasons

children.content: The Queensland premier has opened the state's first major new public hospital in almost a decade — at Springfield, south-west of Brisbane — but it won't offer some services for religious reasons.

image.altText: A hospital exterior, with an entrance sign and a 'mater' sign.

items.title: Big guns return as Matildas call up teen goalkeeper

children.content: Mary Fowler, Ellie Carpenter and Steph Catley are back in the Matildas squad for games against Mexico, while a teenager has been called up.
